candidates.name: Abigail Spanberger
candidates.notes: Former CIA officer. Heritage Action score 5%. Planned Parenthood 100%. Supports assault weapons ban, abortion rights constitutional amendment, terminated ICE cooperation as governor. Won governorship 2025.

profile.background: CIA case officer (2006-2014), US Postal Inspector. US House VA-07 (2019-2025). First woman elected Governor of Virginia.

candidates.name: Ghazala Hashmi
candidates.notes: First Muslim woman elected statewide in US. Killed parental rights bills as committee chair. Sponsored assisted suicide bill. Voted for semi-auto firearms ban (SB1). Authored healthcare for undocumented minors.

profile.education: Georgia Southern University (BA), Emory University (PhD English)
profile.background: Community college professor 30 years. VA State Senator (2020-2026). First Muslim woman elected statewide in US. Sworn in on Quran.

candidates.name: Jay Jones
candidates.notes: First Black VA AG. Catholic but votes against Church teaching on abortion, marriage, guns. Served on Planned Parenthood board. 2025 text scandal: violent remarks about Republican legislators.

profile.education: College of William & Mary (BA Government & History), University of Virginia School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Son of two judges. Assistant AG for DC. VA House of Delegates (2018-2022). First African-American VA Attorney General.

candidates.name: David Goosman
candidates.notes: STRONGEST CHRISTIAN CONSERVATIVE on the board. Baptist deacon who explicitly campaigns on Christian conservative principles. Retired Air Force officer, 21 years military service. Campaign website is goosmangop.org — the 'GOP' is in the URL. Defeated incumbent Kevin Marshall by a wide margin running as the more conservative option. His organization is explicitly 'committed to advancing Christian conservative leadership in Spotsylvania County.' Newly elected — limited voting record but strong faith-based platform.

candidates.name: Gerald Childress
candidates.notes: Marine veteran and Spotsylvania County native. Graduated from Spotsylvania High School. Served on Planning Commission (Chairman), Transportation Committee, and Economic Development Authority before BOS. Explicitly states belief in 'limited government, individual responsibility, and fiscal conservatism.' Initially pro-data-center development but shifted to support SUP requirement. First term — building voting record.

candidates.name: Drew Mullins
candidates.office: Board of Supervisors — Courtland District (Vice Chairman)
candidates.notes: Most decorated military veteran on the board — 23-year Navy SEAL with 14+ deployments. Elected Vice Chairman in Jan 2026 (Frazier was perturbed by the vote). Campaign focuses on economic development, competitive county employee salaries, and public safety — NOT culture war issues. Pragmatic Republican who emphasizes 'healthy county growth.' Small business owner (Azimuth Analytics). UMW graduate pursuing graduate degree at University of Arizona.

candidates.name: Lori Hayes
candidates.office: Board of Supervisors — Lee Hill District (Chairman)
candidates.notes: Board Chairman and independent who caucuses with Republicans. Social work degree from GMU. Co-owned Calypso Kennels in Stafford with her brother (who died in 2023). Strongest 2A score due to proactive leadership on sanctuary reaffirmation. Runs as independent — NOT endorsed through GOP nomination. Supported redistricting referendum opposition (6-0). Pragmatic, growth-management focused. Limited public positions on social/cultural issues — scores lower on life, marriage, and christian_nationalism due to absence of evidence, not contrary evidence.

profile.background: Chairman, Spotsylvania Board of Supervisors, Lee Hill District

candidates.name: Jacob Lane
candidates.notes: Blue-collar Republican — Luck Stone foreman who has been involved in county issues since his 20s. Won Feb 2022 special election after Barry Jett's death. Claims ideological alignment with Jett. Vocally defended the board's redistricting referendum resolution saying it was about 'equal and fair' timing for both sides. One of the most vocal 2A supporters — brought it up proactively during the Jan 2026 meeting. Youngest BOS member at ~40. Infrastructure and growth management focused.

candidates.name: Dr. Deborah H. Frazier
candidates.notes: LONE DEMOCRAT on the board. First African American BOS member in Spotsylvania history. Career educator (25-year principal, now Chief Academic Officer). Abstained from collective bargaining opposition letter (6-0, she abstained) saying she was 'on the fence.' Abstained from redistricting referendum opposition (6-0, she abstained). Was 'perturbed' when Mullins was elected Vice Chair over her or her preferred candidate in Jan 2026. Served on Spanberger's gubernatorial transition team. Notable: she voted YES on 2A sanctuary BOTH times — suggesting some independence from Democratic Party on gun issues in this rural-suburban county. Scored 2 on self_defense because she did support 2A sanctuary. Education score reflects career dedication but from progressive establishment perspective, not parental rights/school choice angle.

candidates.name: Kerry Devine
candidates.notes: Mayor since 2024. Registered Independent but Dem-endorsed. Council under her leadership: LGBTQIA+ Pride Month, DEI commitment, 100% renewable energy resolution. No public stances on abortion, marriage, immigration, or religious liberty. Supports gun giveback program.

profile.education: University of Mary Washington
profile.background: Social studies teacher, Stafford County. City Council since 2004. School Board 1996-2004. Mother of four.

candidates.name: Charlie Frye Jr.
candidates.notes: Most documented progressive on council. Teamster (Local 322), 24-yr UPS driver. Created gun giveback program (200+ guns collected since 2014). Led slave auction block removal. 100% renewable energy by 2035. Progressive Voters Guide "most progressive choice." Member Shiloh Baptist Church but policy positions entirely progressive.

profile.background: Vice Mayor since 2020. Ward 4 Councilman since 2014. Only Black member of council. Gun give-back program. Racial equity advocate.

candidates.name: Jonathan Gerlach
candidates.notes: Attorney and retired archaeologist. NAACP executive board member and Legal Redress Committee. Focuses on workforce development, historic preservation. Avoids single-issue politics. Won Ward 2 with 60%. Almost no culture-war positions documented.

profile.background: Attorney (Gerlach Law Firm PLC). Retired archaeologist. Ward 2 representative.

candidates.name: Jason Graham
candidates.notes: Former Ward 1 rep (term ended Dec 2025). Progressive Voters Guide listed. Voted with council on renewable energy and equity initiatives. No culture-war positions documented.

profile.education: University of Missouri (Journalism), University of Rochester (MBA)
profile.background: Consultant, former Ward 1 councilman. Did not seek re-election.

candidates.name: Joshua Cole
candidates.notes: Supports enshrining reproductive freedom in Virginia Constitution | Progressive on LGBTQ+ issues — expected to vote for marriage equality amendment | Supports gun violence prevention measures

profile.background: Baptist pastor. First Black person elected from Fredericksburg region (2019, age 29). Lost 2021, won again 2023/2025.
profile.education: Liberty University (attended 3 years)

candidates.name: Deuntay T. Diggs
candidates.office: Board of Supervisors — George Washington District (Chairman)
candidates.notes: Runs as Independent but governs in coalition with Democratic members. Law enforcement background provides some conservative credibility, but his social positions, tax votes, and LGBTQ advocacy place him far from Christian conservative alignment. His chairman election depended on Democratic votes.

profile.background: 15-year Stafford County Sheriff's Office veteran, Lieutenant in Special Operations. Board Chairman.

candidates.name: Crystal Vanuch
candidates.office: Board of Supervisors — Rock Hill District
candidates.notes: Strongest conservative on the board by far. Led 2A sanctuary effort. Explicit pro-life statements. Congressional campaign was fully America First platform. Voted against tax increases. Lost 2022 GOP primary for VA-07 to eventual nominee.

profile.education: Virginia Wesleyan University (Magna Cum Laude)
profile.background: Rock Hill District supervisor since 2020. Former Board Chairman. Ran for Congress 2022 on border security, public safety, energy independence.

candidates.name: Tim Kaine
candidates.notes: U.S. Senator since 2013. 2016 VP nominee. Former VA Governor (2006-2010). Catholic but votes progressive on all social issues. F-rated by NRA. 100% NARAL rating.

profile.education: University of Missouri (BA), Harvard Law School (JD)
profile.background: Attorney, former Governor of Virginia, former DNC Chair, 2016 VP nominee
profile.committees: Armed Services; Budget; Foreign Relations; Health, Education, Labor and Pensions (HELP)

candidates.name: Mark Warner
candidates.notes: U.S. Senator since 2009. Former VA Governor. Wealthiest member of Senate (~$300M). RETIRING in 2026 — open seat race. Moderate on business issues but progressive on social issues. F-rated NRA.

profile.education: George Washington University (BA), Harvard Law School (JD)
profile.background: Telecom executive, venture capitalist, former Governor of Virginia
profile.nra_rating: F (downgraded from C after supporting background check and assault weapons legislation)
profile.committees: Banking, Housing and Urban Affairs; Budget; Rules and Administration; Select Committee on Intelligence (Vice Chair)

candidates.name: Rob Wittman
candidates.notes: Served since 2007. Fredericksburg-area district. A-rated by NRA. Strong conservative voting record. Member of House Armed Services Committee.

profile.education: Virginia Tech (BS), University of North Carolina (MPH), Virginia Commonwealth University (PhD)
profile.background: Environmental health specialist, Montross town mayor, VA House member before Congress
profile.committees: Armed Services (Vice Chairman); Subcommittees: Tactical Air and Land Forces; Seapower and Projection Forces

candidates.name: Jen Kiggans
candidates.notes: Navy helicopter pilot veteran. Elected 2022. Virginia Beach district. Nurse practitioner. A-rated by NRA. Pro-life with exceptions. Conservative voting record.

profile.education: U.S. Naval Academy (BS), Old Dominion University (MSN)
profile.background: Navy helicopter pilot, nurse practitioner, former VA state senator
profile.committees: Armed Services; Natural Resources; Veterans' Affairs

candidates.name: Bobby Scott
candidates.notes: Served since 1993. Longest-serving member of VA delegation. Progressive voting record. F-rated by NRA. 100% NARAL rating.

profile.education: Harvard University (BA), Boston College Law School (JD)
profile.background: Attorney, first African American congressman from Virginia since Reconstruction

candidates.name: Jennifer McClellan
candidates.notes: Elected 2023 special election. Former VA state senator. Progressive. First Black woman to represent VA in Congress.

profile.education: University of Richmond (BA), University of Virginia School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Attorney, former VA state senator and delegate, first Black woman in VA congressional delegation
profile.committees: Energy and Commerce (including Communications and Technology Subcommittee)

candidates.name: John McGuire
candidates.notes: Navy SEAL veteran. Elected 2024, defeating Bob Good in primary. Trump-endorsed. Former VA state senator. Very conservative. A-rated by NRA.

profile.education: U.S. Naval Academy
profile.background: Navy SEAL, Green Beret, former VA state senator, small business owner
profile.net_worth: Not publicly available on OpenSecrets (freshman member)
profile.nra_rating: A+ (historically; endorsed by NRA in primary)

candidates.name: Ben Cline
candidates.notes: Shenandoah Valley district. Served since 2019. Freedom Caucus member. Very conservative. A-rated NRA. 0% Planned Parenthood rating.

profile.education: Bates College (BA), University of Richmond School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Attorney, former VA House of Delegates member, Freedom Caucus member
profile.committees: Appropriations; Judiciary; Budget; Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence

candidates.name: Eugene Vindman
candidates.notes: Elected 2024. Twin brother of Alexander Vindman (Trump impeachment witness). Born in Soviet Union. Army veteran. Progressive Democrat.

profile.education: SUNY Binghamton (BA), Georgetown University (JD)
profile.background: Army JAG officer, attorney, brother of Alexander Vindman
profile.net_worth: Not publicly available on OpenSecrets (freshman member)

candidates.name: Don Beyer
candidates.notes: Served since 2015. Arlington/Alexandria district. Former Lt. Governor (1990-1998). Ambassador to Switzerland. Very progressive. F-rated NRA.

profile.education: Williams College (BA)
profile.background: Car dealer, former Lt. Governor of Virginia, former Ambassador to Switzerland
profile.committees: Ways and Means (Subcommittees: Trade; Tax); Science, Space and Technology; Joint Economic Committee (Senior House Democrat)

candidates.name: Morgan Griffith
candidates.notes: SW Virginia coal country district. Served since 2011. Very conservative. A-rated NRA. Strong pro-life, pro-coal, pro-gun record.

profile.education: Emory and Henry College (BA), Washington and Lee University School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Attorney, former VA House majority leader, coal country advocate

candidates.name: Suhas Subramanyam
candidates.notes: Elected 2024. Former VA state senator. Loudoun County area. Progressive Democrat. First Indian American from Virginia in Congress.

profile.education: Northwestern University, Georgetown University Law Center
profile.background: Attorney, former Obama White House tech policy advisor, former VA state senator
profile.net_worth: Not publicly available on OpenSecrets (freshman member)
profile.committees: Oversight and Government Reform (Ranking Member, Military and Foreign Affairs Subcommittee); Science, Space and Technology; Ethics; Congressional-Executive Commission on China

candidates.name: James Walkinshaw
candidates.notes: Won September 2025 special election after Gerry Connolly died May 21, 2025 of esophageal cancer. Former Fairfax County supervisor. Progressive Democrat.

profile.education: B.A. Politics, New York University (2005)
profile.birthplace: Arlington County, Virginia (grew up in Prince William County)
profile.background: Former Fairfax County Board of Supervisors member. Won 2025 special election for VA-11 after Gerry Connolly's death.
profile.net_worth: Not publicly available on OpenSecrets (freshman member)
profile.nra_rating: F (expected; no official grade yet posted for 2025)
profile.committees: Not yet confirmed (seated September 2025 via special election)

candidates.name: Libby Humphries
candidates.notes: Most documented progressive official in Fredericksburg. Cashless bail policy. Attended BLM protests. Supports Fair and Just Prosecution (progressive prosecutors org). Created Therapeutic Docket for mentally ill defendants. Supports felon voting rights restoration. NAACP Education Committee co-chair. Began career as public defender. Member St. George's Episcopal Church. Successfully prosecuted high school shooting (murder convictions) and child abuse (6 life sentences) — not soft on violent crime despite progressive philosophy.

profile.background: Career prosecutor since 1999. Served in Charlottesville (1999-2018) then Fredericksburg (2018-present). Criminal Justice Reform Alliance member.

candidates.name: Ryan Mehaffey
candidates.notes: Marine veteran who enlisted at 17 after 9/11. Exceptional academic record (summa cum laude BBA + JD/MBA in 3 years). 10+ year prosecution track record described as 'tough but fair.' Republican endorsed, ran unopposed. Condemned misleading sample ballots that confused voters — shows election integrity concern. Prosecution philosophy balances public safety with rehabilitation. Victim-focused approach. His office ran a public education program on gun laws — 'arming citizens with knowledge.' Self-identifies as 'Conservative' on campaign website.

profile.background: U.S. Marine veteran (enlisted 2002 after 9/11). Prosecutor with 10+ years trial experience. Father of four.

candidates.name: Eric Olsen
candidates.notes: Strong law-and-order prosecutor with impressive record. Republican but focused on prosecution rather than policy advocacy. Gun violence concern is notable but he addresses it through prosecution, not gun control legislation. Education category largely N/A for this office. Longest-serving current Stafford elected official in this scorecard.

profile.background: 30+ year Stafford prosecutor. VA Prosecutor of the Year 2008. Tried 1,100+ DV cases. Testified before US Senate Judiciary Committee 2017.

candidates.name: Winsome Earle-Sears
candidates.notes: Lt. Governor of Virginia (2022-2026). First Black woman elected statewide in VA. Born in Jamaica. Marine veteran. Conservative but lost 2025 gubernatorial race to Spanberger. Pro-life, pro-2A, pro-border security. Not US-born (Jamaica).

profile.education: Old Dominion University (BA), Regent University (MA)
profile.background: U.S. Marine veteran, small business owner, Lt. Governor of Virginia 2022-2026, first Black woman elected statewide in VA
profile.prev_election_opponent: Hala Ayala (D) in 2021 Lt Gov race

candidates.name: John Reid
candidates.notes: Republican candidate for Lt. Governor 2025. Lost to Ghazala Hashmi. Conservative.

profile.background: Republican Lt. Governor candidate 2025

candidates.name: Jason Miyares
candidates.notes: VA Attorney General 2022-2026. First Hispanic AG in VA history. Son of Cuban immigrant. Strong conservative record as AG: fought Biden vaccine mandates, joined states challenging gun control, defended parental rights policies.

profile.education: College of William & Mary, University of Florida School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Attorney, former VA House of Delegates member, AG of Virginia 2022-2026. First Hispanic statewide official in VA. Lost re-election to Jay Jones 2025.

candidates.name: Jannan Holmes
candidates.notes: Licensed Clinical Social Worker with Women's Studies background. School board 2010-2023. Dem-endorsed. Campaign theme: "all people matter, no exceptions." Focus on equity, mental health, youth access.

profile.background: Elected 2023 at-large. Democrat-aligned.

candidates.name: Will Mackintosh
candidates.notes: UMW history professor (teaches gender history). In same-sex interracial marriage. Endorsed by LGBTQ+ Victory Fund, IBEW Local 26, AFL-CIO. Platform: walkable urbanism, historic preservation, CTE education. Most clearly scoreable on marriage category.

profile.background: UMW professor and historian. Environmental sustainability advocate. Supports carbon-neutral/carbon-negative living.

candidates.name: Susanna Finn
candidates.notes: Newest member, appointed Feb 2025. Navy program manager, certified urban planner. Unanimously chosen by progressive council (ideological alignment). Uses progressive language: "diverse voices," "inclusive," "vibrant communities." No culture-war positions documented.

profile.education: UMW (BA Historic Preservation), VCU (Masters Urban & Regional Planning)
profile.background: Certified planner (AICP). U.S. Navy program manager.

candidates.name: Tinesha Allen
candidates.notes: Limited public positions on national culture-war issues. Focus is on local governance — school funding, infrastructure, competitive pay. Absence from CRT vote is a significant data point. Governs in coalition with Diggs. Score of 1 in each category reflects Democratic affiliation with no contradicting conservative positions found, but also minimal direct evidence of actively opposing Christian conservative positions at local level.

profile.education: Vanderbilt University (Political Science), Shenandoah University (Nursing)
profile.background: Elected Nov 2019. First African American woman elected to Stafford BOS.

candidates.name: Maya Guy
candidates.office: Board of Supervisors, Aquia District (Vice Chair)
candidates.notes: Most evidence comes from school board tenure. Actively pushed equity/DEI agenda in schools — diversity hiring, equity-based admissions changes, equity PD for staff. This record directly conflicts with Christian conservative education priorities. Newly seated on BOS (Jan 2026) so limited supervisor voting record.

profile.background: Former Vice Chairwoman of Stafford School Board. Nearly two decades of community advocacy.

candidates.name: Kecia Evans
candidates.notes: Most documented progressive positions of any BOS member due to 2021 delegate race. Moms Demand Action endorsement is explicit anti-2A marker. Explicit LGBTQ+ rights support. Criminal justice reform focus (reduce mass incarceration). Interesting: Regent University law degree is from Pat Robertson's school, but her positions do not reflect that institution's conservative values.

profile.education: BA Criminal Justice, MS Cybersecurity/Digital Forensics, LLM National Security Law
profile.background: 22+ years criminal justice. 18 years supporting public defenders. Former Chair Stafford Board of Zoning Appeals. NAACP Criminal Justice committee. Stafford resident since 2015.

candidates.name: Pamela Yeung
candidates.notes: More establishment Democrat than progressive firebrand. Focus is on fiscal management (public-private partnerships, revenue diversification) and education infrastructure. Less overtly progressive than Evans or Guy on social issues, but solidly aligned with Democratic caucus on all major votes. Baylor education is notable but does not appear to influence governance.

profile.education: Howard University, Johns Hopkins, Baylor University (Doctorate in Leadership and Organizational Change)
profile.background: College professor. Science/technology consultant. Board Chair 2023. Vice Chair 2022.

candidates.name: Darrell English
candidates.notes: Solid conservative alignment based on voting pattern (voted with Vanuch/Bohmke on tax and leadership votes) and law enforcement career. However, far fewer explicit public position statements than Vanuch. Scores reflect available evidence with moderate conservative inference from party affiliation and voting record.

profile.background: 40+ year Stafford County Sheriff's Office veteran (First Sergeant). Life member Stafford Rescue Squad. Two terms Planning Commission (Vice Chairman 2020). Attended Stafford County schools.

candidates.name: Megan Jackson
candidates.notes: Elected 2023, defeating incumbent Kirk Twigg (57.97% to 34.47%). Twigg was one of the members who called for burning library books. Jackson elected chair 4-3 in Jan 2025; said she hoped to 'say goodbye to the chaos.' Part of the moderate majority.

candidates.name: Belen Rodas
candidates.notes: Elected 2023 as a board critic who cited lack of transparency and conduct issues. Elected vice chair 5-2 in Jan 2025. Ran on platform opposing the prior board's dysfunction.

candidates.name: Dr. Carol Medawar
candidates.notes: Elected 2023, defeating David Ross (longtime Board of Supervisors member). Veteran educator who previously ran for school board in Stafford County. Supported Jackson for chair.

candidates.name: Dr. Lorita C. Daniels
candidates.notes: First elected 2019, re-elected 2023 with 55.6%. Served as Board Chair in 2024. Holds doctorate in public administration from Virginia Tech. Announced campaign for U.S. Senate in Nov 2025. Still serving on school board as of April 2026.

candidates.name: Jennifer Craig-Ford
candidates.notes: Elected Nov 2025 with ~37% in a multi-candidate race. Replaced Nicole Cole, who did not seek re-election. Cole was described as part of the board's liberal wing.

candidates.name: Lawrence A. DiBella III
candidates.notes: Elected Nov 2025 with 58.68%. Replaced April Gillespie. Described as conservative-leaning. Part of a potential new faction on the board.

candidates.name: Richard J. 'Rich' Lieberman
candidates.notes: Elected Nov 2025 with ~53.86%. Replaced Lisa Phelps (conservative). Described as non-partisan centrist.

candidates.name: Dr. Elizabeth Warner
candidates.notes: School board member, Griffis-Widewater.

profile.background: Former Governor of Virginia and telecom venture capitalist who co-founded Nextel. Built fortune in early cell phone industry. Senate Intelligence Committee chairman/vice chair. In Senate since 2009.
profile.education: George Washington University (BA Political Science), Harvard Law School (JD)

candidates.name: Dr. Sarah Chase
candidates.notes: School board member, Falmouth.

candidates.notes: School board member, Aquia.

candidates.name: Kathleen 'Katie' Pomeroy
candidates.notes: Re-elected Nov 2025 with 96.74% (effectively uncontested). Previously served as Vice Chair.

candidates.name: Annie Langdon
candidates.notes: Elected Nov 2025 with 62.39%, defeating Sarah Stelmok. New Ward 3 seat was created when Fredericksburg expanded from 5 to 6 board members. Elizabeth Rehm held the seat by appointment Sep-Dec 2025 after Jennifer Boyd resigned.

candidates.name: Malvina Rollins Kay
candidates.notes: Re-elected Nov 2025 with 78.36%. Longtime board member, has served for over 13 years.

candidates.name: Jarvis E. Bailey
candidates.notes: Re-elected 2023. Longtime board member (13+ years). Previously served as Board Chair. African American community leader in Fredericksburg.

candidates.name: Molly McFadden

candidates.name: Danny Avula

candidates.name: Andrew Breton

candidates.name: Katherine Jordan

candidates.name: Matthew R. Hamel
candidates.notes: Elected 2022. Originally Republican, switched to Democrat Nov 2024. Won re-election Nov 2025 (57% vs 43% over David Mick R).

candidates.name: Shannon E. Glover
candidates.notes: Elected 2020. Re-elected 2024. Portsmouth is one of the most consistently Democratic cities in Virginia. Council is at-large (mayor + 6 members).

candidates.name: William E. Moody Jr.
candidates.notes: Vice Mayor. At-large.

candidates.name: Stephanie N. Morales
candidates.notes: Won 3rd full term Nov 2025 with ~55%. Defeated Nathan Chapman (I) and Steve Heretick (I).
